title: acronychia rugosa
intro: Acronychia rugosa, also known as the New Zealand tea tree or yellow mangrove, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Rutaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds that can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Acronychia rugosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. Acronychia rugosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various uses in traditional medicine and modern applications.
